title: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the proxy file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The proxy file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kube-proxy service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/proxy Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the each worker node. For example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/proxy Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, proxy file ownership is set to root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into each worker node where kube-proxy is running.
2. Run the command: `stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/proxy`
3. Verify that the output shows `root:root`.
4. If the ownership is not set to `root:root`, run the command: `chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/proxy` to remediate the issue. 
5. Confirm compliance by re-running the `stat` command to ensure the ownership is now correctly set to `root:root`.
